label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's tackle this question. So the story is about an employer who's calculating the money owed to Julia Vassilyevna, the governess. The employer starts off saying they agreed on thirty rubles a month, but Julia had said forty. He then deducts various amounts for Sundays, holidays, days Kolya was sick, days she had a toothache, broken teacup, torn jacket, stolen shoes, and some ten rubles he says he gave her. Eventually, he ends up giving her eleven rubles, but Julia doesn't protest. Then he gets angry because she says "merci" and later admits that in other places she got nothing. He then gives her the full eighty rubles, realizing how vulnerable she is. The question is asking why the employer made those criticisms about her work. The options are: A) She neglected her duties. B) He wanted to pay her as little as possible. C) He wanted to make her realize she was being cheated and protest. D) He
